meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Solve Debugging Blocks via Content Creation
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug code? Master the art of content creation to document your logic, solve errors, and build a standout portfolio using Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: When you hit a wall, stop staring at the screen. Shift from passive debugging to active content creation. By documenting your thought process in public, you force clarity on your code. Use Antigravity to visualize logic and Claude Code to refine your implementation, turning every bug into a learning milestone.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks. The terminal screams red. You are staring at a stack trace that feels like an alien language, and the sinking feeling of imposter syndrome begins to set in. Every developer has stood at this precipice, paralyzed by the complexity of their own creation. You aren't failing; you are simply lacking the framework to articulate the solution.

The secret isn't more caffeine; it is content creation. By translating your technical struggle into a pedagogical narrative, you shift your brain from a reactive state to an analytical one. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to dismantle these barriers, transforming your frustration into a high-value portfolio piece that proves you can build, break, and fix anything.

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, define exactly what the code is doing versus what it should do. This step prepares the context for your documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code block and generate a step-by-step logic flow chart. Identify the exact line where the expected output deviates from the actual output.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/app.py --focus 'identify logic error in function X'

steps.title: Draft a 'How-I-Fixed-It' Narrative
steps.description: Write a 200-word explanation of the bug. Writing forces you to internalize the solution and provides content for your blog or portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical blog post excerpt explaining this specific bug, the symptoms, and the underlying architectural cause.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write ./docs/debugging_log.md --content 'Summarize the bug found in function X and the proposed fix'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply the fix based on the logic you just documented, ensuring the code matches your intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code to follow clean architecture principles while implementing the fix for the identified bug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix ./src/app.py --pattern 'apply fix based on logic flow'

steps.title: Validate with Edge-Case Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix doesn't break other parts of your app by generating unit tests for your new logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate three edge-case unit tests for the refactored function to ensure robustness against invalid user inputs.

steps.title: Public Documentation and Commit
steps.description: Finalize your content by adding a 'Learning Log' to your README, linking your bug fix to a public commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional commit message that summarizes the bug, the solution, and the technical lesson learned.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'Fix: [Issue Name] - Resolved via logic refactoring'

pro_tips: Treat every error message as a prompt for a blog post; the more specific the error, the better the SEO for your portfolio.
Use Claude Code to explain 'why' a fix works, not just 'how' to implement it; this builds your mental model for future debugging.
Maintain a 'Bug Journal' in a private repository to track recurring patterns in your coding style.
When stuck, ask Antigravity to explain the code as if you are a junior engineer; the simplification often reveals the hidden bug.
Always commit your documentation alongside your code; your future self will thank you for the context.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, publish your 'Bug Log' to Dev.to or your personal blog. Demonstrating how you solve problems is more valuable to employers than perfect code.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical workflow to turn every frustration into a showcase of your engineering growth. Stop letting bugs stall your progress and start building a portfolio that commands attention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
